chains.notes: Covenant_certificate rows represent the ongoing compliance-certificate lifecycle — they are seeded with test data in migrations/167_covenant_certificate_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them (src/routes/covenant-certificate-chain.ts has only GET, POST/:id transition routes). Not cascade-born. Must be seeded as external/upstream facility-monitoring reality from the lender.

Drawdown rows represent W21 tranches — they are seeded in migrations/133_drawdown_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them. Not cascade-born. Rows must be seeded to reflect the IPP's drawdown requests against a lender facility.

Disbursement UoP cases (W30) are seeded in migrations/151_disbursement_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them. Not cascade-born. Rows must be seeded to represent actual tranche releases awaiting reconciliation.

Loan default rows are AUTO-CREATED by the reserve_account.breached cascade event (src/cascade-rules/lifecycle-sequencing.ts:268, rule ID 'lifecycle.reserve_breach_to_loan_default'). The cascade inserts a row with status='default_flagged' and source_entity_type='reserve_account', auto-deriving facility_name, borrower_party_id/name from the reserve account row.

Credit facility applications (W53) are seeded in migrations/197_credit_origination_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them (src/routes/credit-origination-chain.ts has only GET and transition routes). Not cascade-born. Rows must be seeded to represent credit origination applications.

Loan transfer cases (W61) are seeded in migrations/213_loan_transfer_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them. Not cascade-born. Rows must be seeded to represent secondary-market loan participation transfers.

Security perfection items (W69) are seeded in migrations/229_security_perfection_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them. Not cascade-born. Rows must be seeded to represent the lender's security package items (mortgages, pledges, etc.).

DSCR monitoring test cases (W86) are seeded in migrations/263_dscr_monitoring_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them. Not cascade-born. Rows must be seeded to represent each test period's ratio monitoring on a facility.

Reserve account obligations (W77 — DSRA/MRA) are seeded in migrations/245_reserve_account_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them. Not cascade-born (though reserve breaches fire cascades TO other chains — loan_default). Rows must be seeded to represent the lender's reserve-account requirements on each facility.

SLL KPI compliance cases (W95) are seeded in migrations/281_sll_kpi_chain_seed.sql. No POST/ endpoint creates them. Not cascade-born. Rows must be seeded to represent the ESG KPI monitoring periods on sustainability-linked loans.

Position limit cases are seeded via migration 148_poslimit_chain.sql + 149_poslimit_chain_seed.sql. No API endpoint creates them; they represent external compliance-detected breaches of trader position caps against FSCA Section 41 limits. Initial state is within_limit; first admin action is raise_warning when telemetry detects a threshold crossing.

Best-execution RFQs are seeded via migration 162_best_execution_chain.sql + 163_best_execution_chain_seed.sql. Represents FSCA Conduct Standard 1/2020 compliance on each client RFQ lifecycle. No API creates them; they are fixtures representing external client inquiries the desk must manage end-to-end. Initial state rfq_received; first action solicit_quotes.

Trade reports are seeded via migration 178_trade_reporting_chain.sql + 179_trade_reporting_chain_seed.sql. Represents FMA 2012 FSCA OTC Derivatives Reporting post-trade regulatory obligation. No API creates them; they are created automatically by back-office when trades execute. Initial state report_due; first action generate_report. src/routes/trade-reporting-chain.ts:1-65 documents the FMA Article 5 reporting flow.

Market abuse cases are seeded via migration 194_market_abuse_chain.sql + 195_market_abuse_chain_seed.sql. Represents FMA Chapter X + STOR suspicious transaction reporting. No API creates them; surveillance system detects alerts. Initial state alert_raised; first action triage. Trader role is read-only; write is admin/regulator only per src/routes/market-abuse-chain.ts:51-64.

Algo certifications are seeded via migration 210_algo_cert_chain.sql + 211_algo_cert_chain_seed.sql. Represents FMA/FSCA MiFID RTS-6 pre-deployment governance gate. No API creates them. Initial state registration_submitted; first action begin_review. Trader role read-only per src/routes/algo-cert-chain.ts:58-70.

Counterparty margin cases are seeded via migration 226_counterparty_margin_chain.sql + 227_counterparty_margin_chain_seed.sql. Represents CPMI-IOSCO PFMI margin management. Initial state limit_active; first action issue_warning. No API creates them; they track clearing member collateral adequacy.

Trade allocations are seeded via migration 242_trade_allocation_chain.sql + 243_trade_allocation_chain_seed.sql. Represents DTCC-ITP post-execution institutional trade allocation. Initial state executed; first action prepare_allocation. No API creates them directly; they are born when a block trade executes and requires per-account breakdown.

Benchmark transitions are seeded via migration 270_benchmark_transition_chain.sql + 271_benchmark_transition_chain_seed.sql. Represents ISDA-protocol + bilateral amendment for legacy benchmark cessation. Initial state inventoried; first action assess_impact. No API creates them; sourced from legacy derivative portfolio inventory.

Settlement fails are seeded via migration 260_settlement_fail_chain.sql + 261_settlement_fail_chain_seed.sql. Represents JSE-STRATE CSDR settlement failure tracking and buy-in initiation. Initial state instruction_pending; first action record_fail when settlement fails. No API creates them; they are detected by settlement ops.

Wave 19 procurement RFP chain. Table oe_procurement_rfps requires: id, rfp_number, project_id, participant_id, title, category, capex_estimate_zar (all NOT NULL per migration 128). Seeded via migration 129 with 12 test RFPs. No route POST /create — rows exist only via seed migration.

Wave 23 Insurance claim chain (FSCA Section 38). Table oe_insurance_claim_chain (migration 136) requires: id, claim_number, participant_id, insurer_name, policy_number, cover_type, incident_type, incident_date, asset_description, claim_value_zar, claim_value_tier (all NOT NULL). Seeded via migration 137 with 10 claims spanning all states + tiers. No create POST.

Wave 27 REIPPPP ED commitment monitoring. Table oe_ed_commitments (migration 144) requires: id, case_number, project_id, project_name, bid_window, commitment_type, commitment_label, baseline_value, baseline_unit (all NOT NULL). Seeded via migration 145 with 11 ED commitments spanning all states + tiers. No create POST.

Wave 81 Project Change-Order / P6 Variation Control. Table oe_project_change_orders (migration 252) requires: id, co_number, project_name, variation_tier (all NOT NULL). Seeded via migration 253 with 10 change orders spanning all states + tiers + reportability classes. No create POST — rows only via seed.

Wave 92 Project Risk Register & P6 Schedule-Risk Analysis. Table oe_project_risks (migration 274) requires: id, risk_number, project_name, risk_tier (all NOT NULL). Seeded via migration 275 with 10 risks spanning all states + tiers + classes. No create POST.

Wave 96 IPP Submittal Log & RFI Register / P6 document control. Table oe_submittal_rfi (migration 282) requires: id, submittal_rfi_number, project_id, project_name, workflow_class, priority_class (all NOT NULL). Seeded via migration 283 with 10 submittals spanning all states + tiers + classes. No create POST.

Wave 97 IPP Daily Field Report / Progress Diary / P6 construction day record. Table oe_dfr (migration 284) requires: id, dfr_number, project_id, project_name, workflow_class, priority_class (all NOT NULL). Seeded via migration 285 with 10 DFRs spanning all states + tiers + workflow classes. No create POST.

cascadeBorn.parentChain: (event-agnostic — any chain firing a regulatorInboxSpec-matched event, e.g. regulator.surveillance_alert_raised + 35 others)

cascadePairs.note: Seed an oe_reserve_account_chain row (reserve_required), advance via confirm-funding then through to a breach action that fires reserve_account.breached; assert a oe_loan_default row is auto-INSERTed with status=default_flagged + source_entity_type=reserve_account; then advance loan_default via accelerate. Rule: lifecycle.reserve_breach_to_loan_default (lifecycle-sequencing.ts:266).

Seed an oe_licence_applications row (application_received), advance begin-review → ... → licence_issued so licence_application.licence_issued fires; assert oe_regulator_levies row born with chain_status=levy_assessed, levy_basis=turnover_based, levy_tier=micro; then advance levy_assessment via issue-invoice. Same event also births licence_renewal (seed takes priority for that chain's own test). Rule: lifecycle.licence_issued_to_levy_and_renewal (lifecycle-sequencing.ts:123).

Event-agnostic materializer; not a fixed parent. Drive ONE representative producer (e.g. seed+advance market_abuse_case to file_stor, or trade_report to a reportable transition) so a regulatorInboxSpec-matched event fires, then assert a regulator_inbox row materialized with chain_status=pending and advance via ack. Rule: regulator_inbox.materialize (regulator-inbox.ts:23). Do NOT model as a per-parent pair — pick one deterministic producer.

executionOrder: 1. Add 1 initiation descriptor (ppa_contract_chain) — but treat it as a SPECIAL CASE, NOT a fold into the generic create-via-+New harness. Its create path /api/rbac/invitations/:token/accept is a side-effect of RBAC invitation acceptance (seedRoleScaffold INSERT OR IGNORE oe_ppa_contract_chain, rbac.ts:641), requires a live invitation token + new-account credentials, and is NOT a chain '+ New' button. Either (a) add a bespoke journey: POST create invitation (admin) → accept (token+email+password+full_name) → assert oe_ppa_contract_chain draft row born → advance via execute (needs board_approval_ref + legal_counterparty_ref); or (b) reclassify it to the seedOnly path by seeding an oe_ppa_contract_chain draft row directly. Recommend (a) since it exercises the only real create path; keep it out of INIT_CHAINS matrix-row filtering.

2. Build a SQL seed fixture module tests/browser/fixtures/no-init-seed.sql (or a TS builder) that INSERTs one fresh, uniquely-keyed row per the 73 distinct seedOnly seedTables (85 seedOnly chains collapse onto ~73 tables — note gca-style sharing: grid_code_compliance + gcc_ncr both target oe_grid_code_compliance, so seed two rows differing by chain_status=monitoring vs non_conformance_raised). Populate every NOT NULL column from each chain's seedNotNullColumns example values; suffix all natural keys (case_number/number/id) with a test-run nonce to avoid collisions with existing migration seed rows and across re-runs.

3. Apply the seed fixture against the target DB BEFORE the Playwright run: for BASE=local dev use `wrangler d1 execute open-energy-db --local --file tests/browser/fixtures/no-init-seed.sql`; for prod-smoke, gate behind a dedicated admin seed endpoint or skip prod (see risks). Wire this into global-setup so it runs once per suite, reusing the single ADMIN token (rate-limit discipline).

4. Extend the meridian-journeys harness with a SECOND data-driven mode 'seed-and-advance' that reads the seedOnly list (parallel to INIT_CHAINS): for each seeded chain, the test (a) reuses the ADMIN token via seedToken(), (b) opens the chain's ledger/thread for the seeded row id (resolve id by querying GET list endpoint filtered to the nonce-suffixed key, since there is no '+ New' create step), (c) asserts the row renders in the Meridian ledger UI, (d) fires the chain's firstAdvanceAction through the Thread actbar (use the explicit firstAdvanceAction from seedOnly rather than the forward-biased verb guesser, because several first actions are role-gated — see risks), (e) records create=seeded/advanced into the same journey matrix + 5xx gate.

5. Add 2 deterministic cross-chain cascade journeys for the fixed parent->child pairs: (reserve_account→loan_default) and (licence_application→levy_assessment). Each: seed parent row → advance parent through to the birth-firing transition → poll GET child-list for the auto-born child (assert born + correct initial chain_status + source linkage) → advance child via its firstAdvanceAction. These need multi-step parent advancement (not just firstAdvanceAction) to reach the birth event, so encode the explicit action path to licence_issued / reserve breach.

6. Add 1 representative materializer journey for regulator_inbox: pick a single deterministic producer (recommend market_abuse_case advanced to file_stor, or trade_report to a reportable transition) → assert a regulator_inbox row materialized (chain_status=pending) → advance via ack. Document that regulator_inbox is event-agnostic and intentionally tested through one producer, not enumerated per parent.

7. Run the combined suite against BASE=http://localhost:8787 first (seed + INIT_CHAINS + seed-and-advance + 3 cascade/materializer journeys), confirm the 5xx summary gate stays green and that advanced-count covers all 89 no-init chains (85 seed + 1 init special + 3 cascade-born). Then evaluate prod-smoke applicability (likely local/staging-only because prod has no SQL seed hook).

8. Update the journey matrix summary assertion to expect 89 no-init chains accounted for (seeded|created|cascade-born) and to flag any chain that neither rendered nor advanced, so a future schema/route drift fails loudly.
risks: ppa_contract_chain has NO chain '+ New' create button — its only create path is an RBAC invitation-acceptance side-effect (seedRoleScaffold, rbac.ts:641) requiring a live token + brand-new account credentials. It cannot fold into the generic INIT_CHAINS matrix; mis-treating it as an initiation row will produce a phantom failing journey. Plus it burns the auth rate-limit (account creation = a login-class op).

Many firstAdvanceAction transitions are role-gated, not admin-universal: grid chains require grid_operator (gca request_studies, grid_code_compliance raise_non_conformance, connection_energization submit_program is ipp_developer), market_abuse_case / algo_certification write is admin/regulator only with trader read-only, hse_incident/permit_to_work etc. are esco/esums roles. The harness today reuses ONE admin token; if admin is not in WRITE_ROLES for a given chain the first action returns 403 and the chain looks un-advanceable. Either confirm admin sees all lanes/has write on every chain, or seed per-role tokens (which multiplies logins and risks the 10/5min/IP limiter).

No SQL seed hook exists on prod — seedOnly rows can only be INSERTed via `wrangler d1 execute --local/--remote` or a migration. The seed-and-advance mode is therefore local/staging-only unless a gated admin seed endpoint is added; prod-smoke would skip 85 of 89 chains. Re-running --remote seeds risks polluting prod data.

Two seedOnly chains share a table (grid_code_compliance + gcc_ncr both on oe_grid_code_compliance with different initial chain_status). A single seed row cannot satisfy both journeys; must seed two rows and the harness must select by chain_status, or the second journey will pick up the wrong row.

regulator_inbox is event-agnostic (matches 36+ events via regulatorInboxSpec). There is no single parent; picking the wrong producer event (one whose advance is itself role-gated or invalid-from-state) yields no materialization and a false-negative. The chosen producer must be a transition admin can actually fire end-to-end.

Natural-key collisions: migration seeds already occupy case_number/number/id values (e.g. APP-2026-0001, GCA-TX-2026-0001). Re-using example values in the test fixture will hit UNIQUE constraints or pick up stale rows. Every fixture key must carry a per-run nonce, and the harness must resolve the row id by that nonce (GET list filter), since there is no create-response id to capture.

FK / parent depth: cascade-born children derive parent fields by JOIN at INSERT time (loan_default reads facility/borrower from the reserve account row; levy_assessment reads from licence_application). If the seeded parent row omits the columns the cascade reads, the child is born malformed or the INSERT silently no-ops. Parent seed rows for the 2 cascade pairs must populate the cascade-read columns, not just the table's NOT NULL set.

Cascade timing: births fire through fireCascade fan-out (queues/async stages). The cross-chain journeys must poll the child-list with a bounded retry, not assert synchronously, or the child will appear absent on a fast assertion.

Reaching the birth event requires multi-step parent advancement (licence_application must reach licence_issued; reserve_account must reach a breach). Each intermediate transition may be role-gated or require fields, so the explicit action+payload path must be hard-coded, not left to the forward-biased verb guesser, which could terminal-close the parent before it births the child.

Some advance actions require body fields (ppa_contract_chain execute needs board_approval_ref + legal_counterparty_ref; several -quantum/-tariff actions likely need numeric inputs). The current best-effort advancer only fires no-field actions; field-required first actions will 422 and show as un-advanced unless the harness supplies minimal valid payloads per action.
